abstract: Diniyah education serves as a cornerstone of the educational landscape in Muslim-majority nations, representing a strategic focal point for policies aimed at harmonizing formal schooling with the reinforcement of religious principles. This article explores the historical trajectory, central role, and evolution of Diniyah education policy. By integrating these programs into national educational frameworks, early religious schooling plays a vital role in shaping theological understanding, ethical conduct, and religious identity. The study highlights how these policies not only elevate the quality of formal education but also foster moral fortitude within the broader community. Despite persistent implementation challenges, the findings suggest that supporting Diniyah initiatives is essential for cultivating a cohesive society rooted in spiritual and ethical values.
